payload.summary: All five amendments are technically valid. fr-2-467b53d8 and fr-2-b5ae378d describe the same cancellation-during-backoff regression from different angles; the others identify a real failure-classification regression, a real child-process cleanup defect, and a confirmed test expectation mismatch.

votes.rationale: In the retry-backoff path, cancellation during the tokio::select! leaves `error` unchanged and the subsequent `fail_run_result(&error, ...)` records the prior backend failure class instead of cancellation. The cited test currently fails with `transport_failure` versus `cancellation`. Converting this branch to use `AppError::InvocationCancelled` (or otherwise overriding the failure class before journaling) is the correct fix.

`AppError::failure_class()` now maps every `BackendUnavailable` to `BinaryNotFound`, but that error variant is used for many non-binary conditions: missing `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, OpenRouter probe/auth/network failures, and numerous GitHub HTTP/JSON/configuration failures. Blanket mapping therefore mislabels those paths, and anywhere `failure_class()` drives retry or failure labeling it applies `BinaryNotFound` semantics too broadly. The mapping needs to be narrowed to genuine binary-not-found cases or split into more specific error variants.

This is the same underlying bug as fr-2-467b53d8 and is independently correct. When cancellation fires during retry backoff, the code falls through to terminal failure handling with the previous backend error, so the run is recorded as `transport_failure` instead of `cancellation`. The cited unit test reproduces that regression.

The process backend only signals the direct child PID. There is no process-group setup in the spawn path, and both timeout and cancellation cleanup ultimately use direct-child SIGTERM/SIGKILL handling. That means subprocesses spawned by the backend process can outlive the killed parent and remain orphaned. The tmux adapter already shows the correct process-group kill pattern; process backend needs equivalent group management to make timeout/cancel cleanup reliable.

The cited resume tests still assert 4 stage entries even though the stub is now configured with `with_transient_failure(..., 5)`. With a retry budget of 5 for `TransportFailure`, the failed run consumes 5 stage entries and the resumed success adds a 6th. The targeted resume tests currently fail at the cited assertions with `left: 6, right: 4`, so the test expectations are stale.
